Petition For Authority To Sel Conservatee'S Real Property, To Execute Listing Agreement, To Make Cosmetic Improvements To The Conservatee'S Real Property, And For Allowance To Pay Conservatee'S Grandson
Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillina Ng for and with Christine Witherspoon. Nancy Rasch for and with Claudia B. Richardson. Tommie Jean Bradley was also present in the courtroom.

The Court stated that the matter was on for a petition to sell the property and that it had been continued one week to see if the parties could reach an agreement. Ms. Ng stated that she received a new care plan but it still would not work. Ms. Rasch stated that Ms. Bradley put together a proposal, that it is a "bare bones" budget, that her client wants to remain in her home, and that her client will give up her "Silver" rides which would save about $800.00 per month. In addition, she stated that Ms. Bradley proposed $1,500.00 per month for her services, that she would do the shopping, and that she would pay for her own groceries. In response to the Court, Ms. Rasch stated that Tommie Jean Bradley did not currently reside at the Conservatee's residence. Discussions were held regarding a contract for care which would state that if she is not caring for the Conservatee then she must vacate the premises. Ms. Ng stated that they couldn't pay the amount Ms. Bradley had requested for her services; it would have to be reduced to half. The Court stated that even if the numbers worked that it had concerns with regard to the care plan since it involved the two individuals that caused the conservatorship in the first place. Ms. Rasch stated that Ms. Bradley would accept $680.00 per month as care compensation. Ms. Ng presented additional issues as set forth on the record which included the PG & E, the laundry service, and the $200.00 gas request. Mr. Rasch responded and stated that it comes down to a trade off, that the question is how safe is safe, and that her client adamantly states that she wishes to remain in her home. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Sum.

Petition For Authority To Sel Conservatee'S Real Property, To Execute Listing Agreement, To Make Cosmetic Improvements To The Conservatee'S Real Property, And For Allowance To Pay Conservatee'S Grandson
Off Calendar Jun-15-2009 Continued To Jun-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon. Nancy Rasch for and with Claudia Richardson.

The Court stated that there were notice issues. Ms. Ng addressed the notice issues as set forth on the record and her request to waive notice was granted. Ms. Ng stated that there were loans taken against the property by the grandchildren; roughly $100,000.00 and that at the time of the conservatorship they asked for a RAM (Reverse Ajustable-Rate Mortgage). Ms. Witherspoon stated that the realtor thinks that the property should list for the high $700,000.00. Ms. Ng stated that there are a couple of liens, that she estimates that they may get $100,000.00, that the home care cost is $10,000.00 per month, that they have exhausted everything, that they had wanted the Conservatee in a daycare program because it would have reduced costs, but the Conservatee didn't like the daycare program. In addition, Ms. Ng stated that there were some discussions about the family being care providers but that it hasn't worked out. Ms. Rasch stated that her client adamantly opposes the sale and that they can cut costs by using family members as caregivers. Ms. Witherspoon addressed various issues with the family members providing care, stated that when she arrived the Conservatee had not been to the doctor in a year, and that her medications were all over the house. The Court continued the matter to June 22, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel are to meet, confer, and attempt to see if they can agree on something.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. McDaniels.

Off Calendar May-03-2007 Text Ruling

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatees Trust, For Removal Of Current Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-03-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christine Witherspoon (Petitioner); Lillian Ng (Counsel for Ms. Witherspoon); Claudia Richardson (Trustor); Nancy Rasch (Court-appointed Counsel for Ms. Richardson); Carolyn Espey (Counsel for the Resigning Temporary Trustee, John Daniels); Ruth Dacus (Sister/Interested Party).

Ms. Rasch reported that her client does not oppose the appointment of a Successor-Trustee. Ms. Ng requested authority to get further loan against the property (which was granted by the Court). The Final Accounting by the Resigning Temporary Trustee is on file and is set for hearing on June 6, 2006. The Court granted the Petition to Bring the Trust Under Court Supervision, Removed the Current Trustee, and appointed Christine Witherspoon the Successor Trustee of the Revocable Living Trust of Claudia Richardson (signing the Order for same in open court).

First accounting by the Successor Trustee must be filed no later than two weeks before July 14, 2008.
Bond was fixed at $65,000.00.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Court Investigator: Mary Joy Quinn; Bailiff: R. Del Rosario; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee Filed March 22, 2006
Off Calendar Mar-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christine Witherspoon (Petitioner); Lillian Ng (Counsel for Ms. Witherspoon); Carolyn Espey (Counsel for the Resigning Temporary Successor Trustee); Nancy Rasch (Court-appointed Counsel for Claudia Richardson). Claudia Richardson (Proposed Conservatee/ Trustor) did not appear.

At the outset, Ms. Espey reported that her client wishes to resign as Temporary Conservator and Temporary Trustee. The Court noted that two children exist, but only one address was listed. Consequently, Ms. Ng will need to submit an Amendment. The Court questioned Notice to beneficary, Ronald Richardson. Ms. Espey reported that Mr. Richardson was homeless and could not be located. The Court noted that attempts to locate Mr. Richardson should continue. If he is not located by the time of the General hearing, the Court will dispense with Notice. After Ms. Rasch discussed bond and line of credit, the Court granted the Ex Parte Petition for Court Supervision of the Trust, Suspension of Powers of the Current Trustee, and appointed Christine Witherspoon the Temporary Successor Trustee until May 3, 2007 (signing the Order for same in open court). The Order included a provision that the Temporary Successor Trustee is authorized to withdraw no more than $20,000.00 on the existing line of credit.

The Final Accounting from the Resigning Temporary Successor Trustee (John Daniels) must be filed two weeks before May 3, 2007. No appearance is required if the Final Accounting is filed timely.

Bond was set at $30,000.00.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Arlene Rita Howell CSR#2542; Court Investigator: Mary J. Quinn; Bailiff: F. Smith; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee Filed March 22, 2006
Off Calendar Jan-25-2007 Continued To Mar-08-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch (Court-appointed Counsel for the Proposed Conservatee, Claudia Richardson); Carolyn Espey (Counsel for the Temporary Conservator, John Daniels). Claudia Richardson (Proposed Conservatee) did not appear. John Daniels (Temporary Conservator) did not appear.

At the outset, Ms. Espey requested that both petitions be continued to June 2007 noting that her client was in North Carolina. Ms. Rasch noted that issues with regard to the Conservatorship needed to be resolved much sooner and consequently, requested that the matters be continued to March 8, 2007.

With regard to the trust, the Court questioned Notice to Ronald Richardson and the unresolved issues with Belinda Bradley. Ms. Espey noted that an Ex Parte Waiver of Notice was previously issued for Mr. Richardson.

The Court continued both Petitions to March 8, 2007 extending the Temporary Letters of Conservatorship to that date (which Order for same was signed in open court). The Temporary Trustee Letters do not expire.

Video: Ollie Dacus (Sister/Interested Person) and Charles Dacus Sr. (Brother-in-law/Interested Person) viewed the required Conservatorship video on this date.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Rhonda Fung #4819; Court Investigator: Cynthia Jones; Bailiff: Mizota; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.
